title: rosa canina var. obtusifolioides
intro: Rosa canina var. obtusifolioides, also known as the dwarf dog rose, is a variety of the dog rose (Rosa canina) plant, characterized by its small, thorny stems and fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. obtusifolioides lie in its hips, which are rich in vitamins A and C, as well as antioxidants and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues. Rosa canina var. obtusifolioides is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be consumed as a jam or syrup made from its hips.
